page: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the EASv4 family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz, with 2 threads per core. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with 64.0 GB of memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It includes 131 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the weak category. Memory bandwidth is average for large block read-only operations but weak for small block read-only and large block write operations. Geekbench results show average single-core, image processing, and text processing performance, alongside weak multi-core, software compilation, and ray tracing metrics.

From a resource density perspective, this server offers a high memory allocation relative to its compute footprint, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. The primary tradeoff is the weak multi-core scaling and lower database and web serving throughput, as indicated by weak passmark database operations and static web serving benchmarks.

The E8-4as_v4 is suited for memory-intensive applications with moderate parallel processing requirements. Appropriate workloads include caching layers, small to medium memory-bound databases, single-threaded data processing, and lightweight text or image processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the EASv4 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 16.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 131 GB of local storage. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution, though multi-core performance, multi-threaded decompression, and database operations fall into the lower 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is average for large block reads but weak for writes. This resource profile offers high memory density relative to compute, presenting a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for memory-bound tasks that do not rely on heavy multi-threaded scaling. It is best suited for caching layers, memory-intensive single-threaded applications, and moderate database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 131 GB of local storage, delivering strong single-core but weaker multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 131 GB local storage for memory-bound workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered server offering high memory density and strong single-core performance for memory-constrained, low-parallelism workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB per core
131 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU performance contrasted with weaker multi-core scaling
Suited for memory-bound workloads, caching layers, and single-threaded processing
